title: Asarina filipes
taxonomy: Asarina filipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as the snapdragon family. This classification places it within the order Lamiales. The genus Asarina is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and Asarina filipes is one of its species. Further classification includes its kingdom, Plantae, and its phylum, Angiospermophyta. Asarina filipes is also known by its synonym, Maurandya filipes.

intro: Asarina filipes, also known as climbing snakewort, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) characterized by its twining stems and small, blue-purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Asarina filipes include alkaloids, such as asarin, asarinine, and asarinine acetate, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Asarina filipes is commonly used in herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, as well as for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
